title: M8 — Quality, CI & tooling
description: Make the emulator maintainable: firmware-boot regression tests asserting on serial/display, a Linux+macOS CI matrix, vmstate/savestate for the custom devices, per-device register-coverage docs, and a friendlier run.sh.

title: Firmware-boot regression test
description: Extend tests/ to boot the firmware image and assert on expected serial output and/or OLED framebuffer state, runnable headless in CI.
close_reason: Added tests/boot.sh: headless firmware-boot regression. Boots the firmware (no-SD and, if build/deluge_sd.img exists, with-SD), samples -d int, and asserts 0 data-abort + 0 prefetch-abort exceptions and >=10 IRQs (timer-driven main loop reached). Verified: no-sd 19 IRQs, with-sd 183 IRQs, 0 aborts. Skips cleanly (exit 0) when no firmware image is present, so it is CI-safe. Documented in tests/README.md.

title: CI matrix: build + smoke + regression (Linux+macOS)
description: Update .github/workflows to bootstrap/integrate/build QEMU and run smoke + regression on ubuntu and macos.
close_reason: Extended .github/workflows/build.yml: the existing ubuntu+macos matrix job (renamed build-and-test) now runs tests/boot.sh after tests/smoke.sh. Firmware is not shipped so boot.sh skips cleanly (exit 0) in CI, but the step validates the runner and script on both OSes and is ready to exercise a real boot when DELUGE_FIRMWARE is provided. Ticked the two M8 roadmap boxes.

title: vmstate / savestate for custom devices
description: Add complete vmstate descriptions to the PIC, OLED, SSI, SDHI, timers and SoC so snapshot/savestate round-trips.

title: Per-device register-coverage docs
description: Document which registers each device model implements vs. stubs, under docs/, and keep docs/memory-map.md and roadmap.md in sync.

title: run.sh UX: sd/audio/midi/display options
description: Round out scripts/run.sh with flags for SD image, audio backend, MIDI chardev, and display mode (console/skin/headless).

title: M3 — Storage (SDHI + FatFS)
description: Model the SDHI SD-card host controller backed by a host disk image so the firmware can mount its FAT volume and load songs/samples/presets.
close_reason: Both children done: 5lf.1 SDHI host controller model and 5lf.2 SD disk-image backend + run.sh --sd. Firmware mounts a host FAT32 image via the SDHI controller and reads correct sector data over DMA.

title: SDHI SD host controller model
description: Model the SDHI host (SD_PORT=1) enough for 4-bit SD command/data flow and DMA, exposing an SDBus for QEMU's sd-card device.
close_reason: Implemented rza1l-sdhi device: 16-bit register model (SD_CMD..EXT_SWAP), SDBus exposed for sd-card, command/response (R1/R3/R6/R7 short + R2 CID/CSD frame), SD_BUF0 FIFO for PIO+DMA read/write, SD_INFO1/INFO2 status with inverted-mask IRQ to GIC SPI 273 (INTC 305). Wired into RZ/A1L SoC at 0xE804E800. Builds clean; firmware2 boots with no regression (0 data aborts, 19 IRQ exceptions). Card attach + disk image deferred to 5lf.2.

title: SD disk-image backend + run.sh --sd
description: Attach a host FAT image to the SDHI controller, add a --sd <image> option to scripts/run.sh, and verify the firmware mounts storage (FatFS) and lists the card.
close_reason: SD card attached to SDHI via -drive if=sd/--sd; mksd.sh builds pow2 FAT32 image from sdcard/; firmware mounts FAT32 (super-floppy) and reads correct sector data via DMA. Fixed card-detect (INFO1 0x0020 level), no-response cmds {0,4,15}, and 64-byte DMA window routing (DMA source = reg_base offset 0x00, not SD_BUF0).

title: M6 — Interactive UI skin
description: Build an interactive front panel from the device photo (Synthstrom_Deluge_01.webp): the 8x16 RGB pad grid + 2x8 sidebar, 4x9 buttons, 6 encoders/gold knobs, OLED and 7-seg, all wired to the PIC input/output model so mouse/touch drives the firmware and pad LEDs/displays render live.

title: Front-panel skin from device photo
description: Build a graphical front panel using Synthstrom_Deluge_01.webp as the backdrop, with hit-regions for the pad grid, sidebar, 36 buttons, 6 encoders/gold knobs, OLED and 7-seg.

title: Map skin controls to PIC input events
description: Wire mouse/touch interaction on the skin (pad presses, button clicks, encoder drags/scroll) into the PIC encoder so the firmware responds to the graphical panel.

title: Render pad LEDs, OLED & 7-seg into the skin
description: Composite live decoded pad/sidebar RGB LEDs, gold-knob indicators, the OLED framebuffer and the 7-seg display onto the skin for a faithful visual.

title: M7 — MIDI & USB
description: Expose the MIDI UART (SCIF0) through a QEMU chardev/backend and model the USB host/function controller for USB-MIDI and mass-storage use.

title: MIDI UART (SCIF0) via QEMU chardev
description: Expose the MIDI UART (SCIF0, DMA TX11/RX13, gate timing ch14) through a QEMU chardev so host MIDI I/O reaches the firmware.

title: USB host/function controller model
description: Model the RZ/A1 USB 2.0 controller (USB200 0xE8010000 / USB201 0xE8207000) sufficiently for USB-MIDI and/or mass-storage class use.

title: M2 — Timers, DMA & system glue
description: Model the time base and data-movement peripherals the firmware relies on: OSTM/MTU2 timers (system fast/slow/superfast ticks), the DMAC used for UART/SSI/SPI chains, GPIO for board signals and jack detect, plus ADC/RTC stubs.
close_reason: All 5 children complete: OSTM, MTU2, DMAC, GPIO timers/data-movement models plus ADC+RTC stubs. The firmware's time base, DMA chains, GPIO board signals, battery sense and RTC all run without faulting.

title: OSTM timer model (0xFCFEC000 / 0xFCFEC400)
description: OSTM timer model (OSTM0 0xFCFEC000, OSTM1 0xFCFEC400). NOW BOOT-BLOCKING after SPIBSC: firmware reads OSTMnCNT (counter, offset 0x04; observed read @0xFCFEC004) in a delay/timing loop, catch-all reads 0 forever. RZA1/ostm/ostm.c getTimerValue reads OSTMnCNT. Regs: CMP 0x00, CNT 0x04, TE 0x10, TS 0x14 (start), TT 0x18 (stop), CTL 0x20 (0=interval/down, mode bits). Minimal unblock: free-running CNT off virtual clock like MTU2 (use ptimer or qemu_clock); honour TS/TT start/stop and CTL up/down mode. Two channels.
close_reason: Implemented OSTM model (src/hw/timer/rza1l_ostm.c): two channels, free-running 32-bit up-counter off virtual clock @33.33MHz; TS/TT start/stop, TE status, interval mode down-counter. Unblocked the scheduler's OSTMnCNT poll; boot advanced 3266->4036 PCs. Commit e62fd90.

title: MTU2 timer model (0xFCFF0000)
description: Model the MTU2 channels (mtu2_iodefine.h) that back the Deluge system timers: FAST 528 kHz (ch2), SLOW 32 kHz (ch4), SUPERFAST 33.792 MHz (ch1), MIDI gate (ch2). Drive the firmware scheduler/tick and raise GIC interrupts.
close_reason: Minimal free-running MTU2 model implemented (src/hw/timer/rza1l_mtu2.c): all TCNT_n + TCNTS advance off the virtual clock (~33.33MHz); writes re-base. Unblocked the firmware boot delay loop. Compare-match/interrupt support deferred until needed (would depend on e5c.5 GIC). Commit e4a9997.

title: DMAC model (0xE8200000)
description: DMAC model (0xE8200000). NOW BOOT-BLOCKING (M1): after MTU2, the firmware starts a DMA transfer and busy-waits on CHSTAT (channel-status, block offset 0x24; observed at 0xE82004A4) for completion forever (catch-all reads 0). Each channel block is 0x40 bytes / 16 u32 regs: N0SA,N0DA,N0TB,N1SA,N1DA,N1TB,CRSA,CRDA,CRTB,CHSTAT(0x24),CHCTRL(0x28),CHCFG(0x2c),CHITVL,CHEXT,NXLA,CRLA. Minimal unblock: on CHCTRL SETEN, perform the N0SA->N0DA copy of N0TB bytes (honour CHCFG size/inc), set CHSTAT END/TC and clear EN. Used by OLED SPI TX (RSPI), SSI audio, SD. Map over io.mid catch-all like rspi0/mtu2.
close_reason: Implemented synchronous DMAC model (src/hw/dma/rza1l_dmac.c): 16 channels, on CHCTRL SETEN copies N0TB bytes N0SA->N0DA honouring SAD/DAD increment + SDS/DDS unit sizes, reports END|TC. DSTAT_* computed from CHSTAT. Unblocked the firmware DMA-completion poll; boot advanced 1079->1458 PCs. Link/repeat/HW-request/IRQ deferred. Commit 7a01199.

title: GPIO model (0xFCFE3004)
description: GPIO model (base 0xFCFE3000; macro GPIO @0xFCFE3004). NOW BOOT-BLOCKING after OSTM: firmware busy-polls PPR1 (port pin read, observed 16-bit read @0xFCFE3204, 120k+ times) waiting on input pin state; catch-all returns 0 forever. Also many config writes seen to PIBC/PBDC/PM/PMC/PFC port regs (e.g. offsets 0x360c/0x3614/0x3a0c/0x720c, 16 each) which are harmless once GPIO accepts them. Struct st_gpio in RZA1/system/iodefines/gpio_iodefine.h: P1..P11 @0x00 (output data, 16b), PSR1..11 @0x44 (32b set/reset), PPR0..11 @0x200 (input pin read, 16b; PPR1@0x204), PM @0x300 (mode), PMC @0x400, PFC @0x500, PFCE @0x600, PIBC @0x4000, PBDC @0x4100, PIPC @0x4200. Minimal unblock: store written port regs in a regs shadow; make PPR_n read back the corresponding P_n output latch (loopback) or a sensible default so the pin-state spin exits. Need to find WHICH pin the boot loop waits for (decode the PC reading 0xFCFE3204) to pick the right return value.
close_reason: Implemented minimal GPIO model (src/hw/gpio/rza1l_gpio.c, struct base 0xFCFE3004): shadow all register writes; PPR_n reads loop back the matching P_n output latch (driven pins read high, undriven inputs read 0). Eliminated the 120k+ PPR1 busy-wait spam; firmware now runs its main input-polling loop (encoders/buttons/detect lines) cleanly. Boot holds at 4036 distinct PCs, zero exceptions. Commit b56e4e6.

title: ADC + RTC stubs
description: Stub the ADC (0xE8005800, battery voltage on P1.13) and RTC (0xFCFF1000) with plausible read values so firmware polling does not fault.
close_reason: ADC (0xE8005800) and RTC (0xFCFF1000) stubbed as sysbus devices under src/hw/misc. ADC: ADCSR always reports conversion-ready, ADDRF returns ~3.7V battery reading so inputRoutine drives the battery LED solid (verified: 33 inputRoutine iterations, 0 batteryLEDBlink, 0 faults). RTC: register file with fixed BCD date/time, accepts writes. No unimplemented ADC/RTC accesses; 0 data aborts with and without SD.

title: M5 — Audio (SSI/I2S)
description: Model SSI0 (I2S, 0xE820B000) wired to QEMU's audio backend for real-time output and input, including the codec config path (RIIC/SCUX) the firmware programs.

title: SSI/I2S device model (0xE820B000)
description: Model SSI0 (ssif_iodefine.h): SSICR/SSIFCR/SSIFTDR/SSIFRDR, I2S framing, 44.1 kHz / 32-bit, TX FIFO fed by DMA ch6 and RX by ch7, with FIFO interrupts to the GIC.

title: Audio output to QEMU audio backend
description: Pipe SSI TX samples to QEMU's audio subsystem for real-time playback; add an audio option to run.sh.

title: Audio input path (line/mic)
description: Feed QEMU audio capture (or silence/file) into the SSI RX FIFO so the firmware's input/resampling path runs.

title: Audio codec config stub (RIIC/SCUX)
description: Stub the I2C/RIIC codec configuration and SCUX (0xE8208000) registers the firmware writes during audio bring-up so they don't fault.

title: M1 — Boot & early SoC init
description: Get the DelugeFirmware image executing on -M deluge: load the image, satisfy the firmware's earliest hardware init (clocks, SDRAM, interrupt controller, L2 cache) so it reaches main loop without faulting, and emit real serial output.
close_reason: M1 complete: all 10 children closed. Firmware loads, runs clocks/SDRAM/INTC/L2 init, and reaches its main input-polling loop with 4036 distinct PCs and zero exceptions. SCIF UART channels wired to -serial backends; firmware drives them with PIC/MIDI binary protocols (no human-readable debug text by design). Peripheral catch-alls (io.low/mid/high) now quiet for the modelled regions.

title: Load firmware image and reach the reset vector
description: Support -kernel for both the ELF and the raw deluge-c1_2_1.bin (load to SRAM 0x20000000 / SDRAM 0x0C000000 per linker_script_rz_a1l.ld; entry symbol 'start'). Confirm the CPU fetches the firmware's first instructions under -d in_asm.
close_reason: Board loads ELF (load_elf) or raw .bin (load_image_targphys -> SRAM 0x20000000) with a reset hook that sets the firmware entry PC. Verified: CPU executes from 0x20000000 and reaches the firmware reset_handler at 0x2007b620 under -d in_asm.

title: SPIBSC SPI multi-I/O flash controller (0x3FEFA000)
description: SPIBSC SPI multi-I/O bus controller @0x3FEFA000 (in io.low window). NOW BOOT-BLOCKING after DMAC: firmware busy-waits on CMNSR (common status, offset 0x48; observed read @0x3FEFA048) for TEND (bit0) on each SPI flash command, catch-all reads 0 forever. Drives the serial NOR/QSPI flash holding firmware+samples; also supports memory-mapped read mode (the 0x18000000 SPI ROM window, RZA1L_SPI_ROM_BASE). Minimal unblock: model CMNSR.TEND always set (transfer complete) + accept SMCR/SMENR/SMCMR/SMADR/SMWDR command regs, optionally returning SMRDR data from a backing flash image. Register order: CMNCR,SSLDR,SPBCR,DRCR,DRCMR,DREAR,DROPR,DRENR,SMCR,SMCMR,SMADR,SMOPR,SMENR,...,CMNSR@0x48.
close_reason: Implemented minimal SPIBSC model (src/hw/ssi/rza1l_spibsc.c): CMNSR.TEND always set, SMRDR reads 0 (flash WIP clear -> busy-wait loops exit), SSLF tracks SMCR.SSLKP/DRCR.SSLN. Unblocked the flash status/command polls; boot advanced 1458->3266 PCs. No backing flash device yet (storage milestone). Commit ee075a7.

title: CPG clock pulse generator model (0xFCFE0010)
description: Model enough of the CPG (cpg_iodefine.h) for the firmware's clock setup (I:G:B:P1:P0 = 400/266/133/66/33 MHz from 13.33 MHz) to complete without faulting; accept FRQCR and standby-clearing writes.
close_reason: CPG modelled (src/hw/misc/rza1l_cpg.c) at 0xFCFE0010: shadows FRQCR/FRQCR2 clock-config and STBCR1..10 module-standby block; CPUSTS reads 0. Accepts firmware clock setup + standby-clearing writes without faulting. Boot 4036 PCs, 0 exceptions. Commit 3adc593.

title: BSC / SDRAM controller model (0x3FFFC000)
description: Model the Bus State Controller (bsc_iodefine.h) so SDRAM init for CS3 (external memory 0x0C000000-0x10000000) succeeds; back it with the SDRAM RAM region created by the SoC.
close_reason: BSC modelled (src/hw/misc/rza1l_bsc.c) at 0x3FFFC000: shadows CSnBCR/CSnWCR/SDCR/RTCSR/RTCOR and the SDRAM mode-set windows (+0x1040/+0x2040). SDRAM backed by existing SoC RAM region at 0x0C000000. io.low catch-all now empty; boot 4036 PCs, 0 exceptions. Commit pending.

title: STB standby + WDT watchdog stubs (0xFCFE0000)
description: Provide module-standby (STB) and watchdog (WDT) register stubs so the firmware can ungate peripheral clocks and kick/refresh the watchdog without faulting.
close_reason: STB standby covered by CPG device (STBCRn live in st_cpg block); WDT modelled separately (src/hw/misc/rza1l_wdt.c) at 0xFCFE0000: keyed WTCSR/WTCNT/WRCSR writes accepted, counter never expires so watchdog refresh loop runs clean. Commit 3adc593.

title: INTC -> Cortex-A9 GIC wiring (0xE8201000)
description: INTC -> Cortex-A9 GIC wiring (INTC 0xE8201000). NEXT FOUNDATIONAL step: firmware reached its main loop (4036 PCs) and now touches INTC during init (writes 0xE8201184/0x201530/0x2011a4 = priority/config regs). The bare cortex-a9 has no GIC; INTC programming hits the io.mid catch-all. To deliver interrupts (timers, SCIF, SSIF, USB) need a GIC. Likely approach: switch the SoC from a raw ARM_CPU cortex-a9 to a9mpcore (TYPE_A9MPCORE_PRIV: built-in GIC + private timers + SCU at PERIPHBASE) OR add a standalone GIC, then map the RZ/A1 INTC registers (st_intc: ICDISER/ICDICER enable, ICDIPR priority, ICDIPTR target, ICCICR/ICCPMR cpu iface) onto the GIC distributor/cpu-iface. RZ/A1 INTC is essentially a PL390/GIC v1 - the register layout may map closely to QEMU's arm_gic. Blocks M2-M7 (any interrupt-driven subsystem). Other scattered post-main-loop accesses (lower priority): SCIF1 UART 0xE8007800, SSIF audio 0xE820B000, USB200 0xE8010000.
close_reason: Wired QEMU arm_gic as the RZ/A1 INTC (GICv1): dist @0xE8201000, cpu iface @0xE8202000, revision 1, 1 cpu, 608 irqs; GIC IRQ/FIQ -> Cortex-A9. Register layout matches so mapped directly (no shim). Firmware INTC init now absorbed by the GIC; boot healthy at 4036 PCs, zero exceptions. Provides interrupt foundation for M2-M7. Commit 4e60219.

title: PL310 L2 cache controller (0x3FFFF000)
description: Instantiate QEMU's PL310 at 0x3FFFF000 (already selected in Kconfig) so cache init writes are absorbed.
close_reason: PL310 instantiated from QEMU built-in l2x0 at 0x3FFFF000 (src/hw/arm/rza1l_soc.c). Firmware L2 cache-init writes absorbed (offset 0x1ff000 gone from io.low catch-all). Boot 4036 PCs, 0 exceptions.

title: SCIF UART model with real serial I/O
description: Flesh out the SCIF stub (scif_iodefine.h; SCIF0 0xE8007000 .. SCIF7 0xE800A800) into a working 8-channel UART: FIFO TX/RX, status/control regs, baud, RX/TX interrupts to the GIC, and a CharFrontend backend so early firmware debug appears on -serial. Channel 0 = MIDI, channel 1 = PIC.
close_reason: SCIF stub now a working single-channel UART (src/hw/char/rza1l_scif.c): sync TX to chardev (TDFE|TEND always ready), interrupt-driven RX (RDF|DR + RXI on RIE). Channels 0(MIDI)/1(PIC) instantiated at 0xE8007000/0xE8007800, wired to -serial, RXI->GIC SPI 191/195. All SCIF accesses absorbed; boot 4036 PCs, 0 exceptions. Commit 61f2108.

title: RSPI0 model (OLED/CV SPI, 0xE800C800)
close_reason: Implemented minimal always-ready RSPI0 model (src/hw/ssi/rza1l_rspi.c): reports SPSR.SPTEF|TEND set, accepts+discards SPDR writes, shadows config regs. Unblocked the firmware OLED/CV SPI busy-wait. Actual OLED pixel/CV DAC output via SPI+DMA deferred to M4. Commit 1e5f283.

title: SoC memory map: uncached mirrors + peripheral catch-alls
close_reason: rza1l_soc.c now models the RZ/A1 +0x40000000 uncached RAM mirrors (SRAM 0x60000000, SDRAM 0x4C000000) as aliases and installs three create_unimplemented_device catch-alls (io.low 0x3FE00000, io.mid 0xE8000000, io.high 0xFCFE0000). Eliminated all early data aborts; firmware runs 1000+ distinct PCs and every peripheral poke is observable via -d unimp. Commit fd4d2f1.

title: Boot stalls in audio/SPIBSC init before PIC firmware-version query
description: Firmware never reaches PIC::requestFirmwareVersion() (deluge.cpp:784) within 25s of boot; it loops earlier in AudioEngine::init / audioFileManager.init / initSPIBSC (deluge.cpp:767-781). Only the first PIC flush (10 config bytes incl SET_UART_SPEED 225) reaches the PIC over SCIF1. Consequence: the nj4.1 PIC firmware-version handshake (245 -> [245,version]) is implemented and the DMA RX-ring delivery primitive is unit-correct, but cannot be exercised end-to-end until this init path completes. Boot coverage is 4036 distinct PCs / 0 exceptions (unchanged from pre-PIC baseline). Investigate which init (SSIF/codec, SD/audioFileManager, or SPIBSC flash backing) spins, likely a status poll on an unmodelled peripheral. Blocks validating nj4.x PIC input/version paths.
close_reason: DMAC now raises a per-channel transfer-end interrupt (edge pulse) on completion when CHCFG.DEM is clear, wired to the GIC at SPI 41+ch-32. The firmware's PIC TX-complete ISR (PIC_TX_INT_TrnEnd -> tx_interrupt) now runs and clears txSending, so the UART TX queue drains. requestFirmwareVersion's byte 245 now reaches the PIC and the version handshake completes; PIC stream advanced from ~10 to 499 bytes, boot 4036->4069 distinct PCs, 0->5 IRQ deliveries.

title: M4 — Input PIC & display
description: Model the Deluge PIC coprocessor (SCIF1 serial link) that scans the pad/button/encoder matrix and drives the RGB pad LEDs, gold-knob indicators, 7-segment display and the 128x48 OLED. Decode CPU->PIC commands, synthesize PIC->CPU input events, and render the displays to a QEMU console.
close_reason: Closed

title: PIC coprocessor device on SCIF1 (link + framing)
description: Create the PIC model attached to SCIF1 (initial 31250 bps, full-speed 200000 bps; DMA TX10/RX12). Implement the byte-stream framing, baud switch (cmd 225) and firmware-version query (245). Refs: deluge/drivers/pic/pic.h.
close_reason: PIC modelled as a chardev backend on SCIF1; CPU->PIC bytes flow via TX DMA+SCIF, PIC->CPU via a new DMAC receive-ring push primitive on ch12. Byte-stream framing skeleton with SET_UART_SPEED(225) baud-switch and REQUEST_FIRMWARE_VERSION(245) response implemented. Boot 4036 PCs/0 exceptions, smoke OK. Full per-command framing=nj4.2. Version handshake unreachable end-to-end until delugemu-hv9 (audio/SPIBSC init stall) is fixed.

title: Decode CPU->PIC command stream
description: Decode outbound PIC commands: pad colours (1-9, 48B), per-LED on/off (188-215 / 152-179), gold-knob indicators (20-21), 7-seg update (224), scroll buffers (228-242), dimmer/timing (243-244), and OLED control (247-251 ENABLE/SELECT/DC). Maintain LED/display state for the renderers.
close_reason: deluge_pic.c decodes the full CPU->PIC command set with correct per-command payload lengths, keeping the stream framed for all commands. Maintains display state for the renderers: pad_grid[18][8] RGB, led_on[36], seven_seg[4], gold_knob[2][4], and OLED enable/select/DC control lines. Verified end-to-end against the firmware2 debug ELF (colour blocks, 7-seg, gold knobs and the version handshake all frame correctly; 0 aborts; boot reaches the main loop and OLED rendering). Old blob unchanged.

title: Synthesize PIC->CPU input events
description: Generate inbound events: pad/button press (index 0-179), release (252 + index), boot/reset (129/175), firmware-version (245+byte), idle heartbeat (254). Model the 8x16 pad + 4x9 button matrices and 6 encoders. Refs: deluge/hid.
close_reason: Closed

title: Host input mapping (keyboard/gamepad -> events)
description: Map host keyboard/gamepad input to pad/button/encoder events fed into the PIC encoder, so the firmware is controllable headlessly before the graphical skin exists.
close_reason: Closed

title: OLED display device (128x48 SSD130x) to console
description: Render the OLED (SSD130x command set, 128x48, top pixel offset 5) driven via RSPI0 through the PIC DC/SELECT lines. Maintain a framebuffer and draw to a QEMU graphic console. Refs: RZA1/oled, deluge/drivers/oled.
close_reason: OLED 128x48 renders to QEMU graphic console; whole pipeline working end-to-end

title: 7-segment numeric display rendering
description: Render the 4-char numeric display updated via PIC cmd 224 (seven_segment.h) as an alternative/secondary console readout.
close_reason: Closed

title: RGB pad-grid LED rendering
description: Render the 8x16 main pad grid + 2x8 sidebar RGB LEDs (144 LEDs) from decoded PIC colour/LED state to a console, as the headless precursor to the graphical skin.
close_reason: Closed
